description: Defines naming conventions across frontend, API boundary, backend internals, database, and routes.

rules.name: Frontend uses camelCase
rules.description: Frontend JavaScript variables, functions, request payload fields, response payload fields, and query parameters must use camelCase.

rules.name: Backend internals use snake_case
rules.description: Python variables, methods, functions, DTO properties, domain object properties, and database fields must use snake_case internally.

rules.name: Routes use kebab-case
rules.description: URL path segments must use kebab-case.

rules.name: Constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
rules.description: Constants in frontend and backend code must use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

rules.name: Mapping boundary owns naming conversion
rules.description: camelCase API payloads must be converted to snake_case internal objects at the mapper/contract boundary, and snake_case internals must be converted back to camelCase only at the API response boundary.

rules.name: Entity and module names should remain semantically consistent
rules.description: Singular/plural naming, module ownership, and entity terminology should remain consistent across frontend, backend, routes, repositories, DTOs, and database structures.
